calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Off Calendar

Petition For Instructions To Confirm Appointment Of Successor Trustee Comerica Bank
Off Calendar Jun-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Denise Chambliss for Comerica Bank.

The Court adopted it's tentative ruling as follows: Successor Trustee Comerica Bank's Petition for Instructions to Confirm Appointment of Successor Trustee Comerica Bank is granted. The January 18, 2018 document signed by four beneficiaries purporting to exercise their right under section 4.2(a) of the second amendment to remove and replace the trustee depends for its legitimacy on each of the four signatories being an "income beneficiary" within the meaning of that section. If any one signatory to that document is not an income beneficiary, then the document lacks signatures of a majority of the income beneficiaries. The Court concludes that Marie A. Sorci is not an income beneficiary within the meaning of the trust or Probate Code section 16325. None of what the trust gives to Ms. Sorci satisfies the statutory definition of "a person to whom net income of a trust is or may be payable." Unlike the other six beneficiaries, Ms. Sorci has no right to share in the income or ultimate distribution of the principal of the trust. Her sole right as set out in sections 7.5 and 7.6(a) is to receive certain personal effects and to live rent free in the trust's real property until she dies or decides to relinquish residency. While the trust is required to pay certain expenses related to the property, Ms. Sorci is not entitled personally to receive any payments from the trust during her residency. Nor does the trust entitle her to receive any share of the proceeds from the sale of the property after she no longer resides there. Nor does the trust give her any estate in the trust's real property that would entitle her to lease it to others or otherwise convert its value to personal income for her use. Neither of the proposed hypothetical situations advanced by Respondent in support of including Ms. Sorci as an income beneficiary changes this analysis. The hypotheticals are neither reasonably likely to occur nor provided for under the terms of the trust. Moreover, the trust evidently uses the term "income beneficiary" as a limiting subcategory of all beneficiaries. In several places, when the trust means to include all beneficiaries, such as, for example, the time to object to an accounting (section 4.6) or the limitation on trustee liability (section 4.10), the trust uses the unmodified term "beneficiaries." It is a standard principle of interpretation that each word in a document is to be given meaning and that the drafter's use of different words or phrases has significance. The only person who could be among all beneficiaries but not an income beneficiary is Ms. Sorci. Because Ms. Sorci is not an income beneficiary, the remaining three signatories to the January 18, 2018 document did not constitute a majority of the six total income beneficiaries; therefore, the document failed to effect a removal or appointment of a trustee.

Ms. Chambliss to prepare a form of order that conforms to the tenative ruling.

Judge Peter J. Busch., Clerk: N. Wong, Bailiff: V. Jung. Not Reported.

Petition For Instructions To Confirm Appointment Of Successor Trustee Comerica Bank
Off Calendar May-15-2018 Continued To May-30-18 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Denise Chambliss for and with Petitioner, (Michael Zaglool, vice-president of Comerica Bank)
Andrew Calvert for and with Sara Cyl-Champlin (Respondent)
Rocky Rodriquez and Rosanna Rodriquez also present in court

Mr. Calvert stated that he would be more comfortable with parties debriefing, and cited probate code 16325. Ms. Chambliss stated that she is fine with further briefing. The Petition for Instructions to Confirm Appointment of Successor Trustee Comerica Bank is continued to May 30, 2018 at 2:30 p.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Calvert's brief to be filed by May 21, 2018, and Ms. Chambliss' reply filed by May 24, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
